title: chenopodium album subsp. pedunculare
intro: Chenopodium album subsp. pedunculare, also known as lamb's quarters, is a subspecies of the goosefoot plant, characterized by its edible leaves and stems. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, inflammation, and respiratory problems due to its high content of vitamins A and C, and minerals such as potassium and iron. The health benefits of lamb's quarters include reducing fever, alleviating cold symptoms, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and can also be consumed raw in salads or cooked as a vegetable, making it a valuable addition to herbal medicine and a nutritious food source.
